key_insight: The paper proves well-posedness and demonstrates finite element simulation of a stationary drift-diffusion MEMS discharge model with exponentially field-dependent ionization.
engineering_relevance: It provides a mathematically grounded solver framework for predicting electrical breakdown and charge transport in MEMS devices.
method: Couple Poisson’s equation with continuity equations for positive ions and electrons.
Regularize and truncate nonlinear source terms, then apply Browder-Minty monotone operator arguments.
Use energy estimates and Stampacchia-type truncation to prove bounded weak solutions, then solve with FEM in 2D and axisymmetric 3D geometries.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports analytical existence and uniform boundedness results plus numerical simulations across several geometries, but no quantitative benchmark metrics.
watch_item: The Townsend-type exponential ionization term is highly nonlinear, so stability and accuracy may depend strongly on regularization and mesh resolution.
paper_title: Analysis and Numerics of a Stationary Drift-Diffusion Model for Electrical Discharge in MEMS
